definition: Lawfulness or the way things proceed according to dhamma rather than according to personal wish.
notes: The repository keeps lawfulness as the default because it coordinates cleanly with idappaccayatā, dhammatthiti, and dhammaniyāmatā without importing essence-language. The Buddhadasa usage profile and the Idappaccayatā practical talk profile both support using dhammatā as part of the natural-law support layer around dhamma and conditionality. Nature of things remains an explanatory alternate when the local prose is less technical, but the entry should not be turned into a hidden-substance term.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and support-layer contexts
In explanatory prose where the practical sense of how things work is foregrounded
context_rules.notes: Use only when the sentence remains clearly anti-essentialist.

authority_basis.scope: Supports natural-law handling of the dhamma family.
Supports using dhammatā as part of the natural-law support layer around conditionality and dhamma.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'lawfulness' as the default rendering unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not turn the term into essence-language or a free-floating metaphysical principle detached from conditionality and dhamma-family usage.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift into essence-language or vague mystical 'nature' talk.
